chunk_summary: Tele-nutrition supports anemia management through severity classification, follow-up scheduling, and escalation for non-response, adhering to national guidelines for safe supplementation and stop rules.
learning_objectives: Understand anemia severity thresholds and their implications for tele-nutrition management.
Learn safe iron supplementation protocols for children with mild to moderate anemia.
Identify telemedicine stop rules and escalation criteria for severe anemia or non-response.
key_facts: Daily elemental iron dosing for children 6 months to 5 years with mild/moderate anemia: 3 mg/kg/day for 2 months.
Follow-up every 14 days to monitor adherence, tolerance, and side effects, with repeat hemoglobin testing post-treatment.
Severe anemia thresholds: <7 g/dL in young children, urgent referral required.
Facility-level transfusion criteria: hemoglobin ≤4 g/dL, or 4–6 g/dL with shock, impaired consciousness, heart failure, or dehydration.
Stop rules for tele-nutrition: acute illness, SAM, or known hemoglobinopathy.
reference_quote: Clinical symptoms suggesting cardiopulmonary compromise (tachycardia at rest, breathlessness, syncope, marked lethargy) require urgent in-person assessment rather than remote supplementation.
document_key_topics: Telemedicine stop rules and escalation criteria
